name: United Kingdom (Civil Air Ensign)

colors.symbolism: 'air force blue' field, denoting civil aviation
the dark blue cross and the canton
the fimbriation of the cross and details of the canton
the red details of the Union canton

description: An air-force-blue field bearing a dark blue cross fimbriated white extending to the edges, with the Union Flag in the canton.
history: The British Civil Air Ensign was instituted by an Order in Council on 11 August 1931 for use at civil aerodromes and by civil aviation authorities. Its air-force-blue field with a dark blue cross distinguishes it from the military RAF Ensign.

features.role: dark blue cross fimbriated white, extending to the edges
features.symbolism: a cross throughout dividing the air-force-blue field
the Union Flag, marking the ensign as British
